steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student wants to provide an example of an epistolary novel. To do that, a choice must (1) name a specific novel and (2) explicitly identify it as epistolary. Without both elements, the reader cannot tell that the example demonstrates the concept.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — "In 2019, American author Ocean Vuong published a novel of letters sent from a young man named Little Dog to his mother, Rose.": This names the author and describes letters, but never calls the novel epistolary. A reader unfamiliar with the term would not connect the example to the concept. ✗
  • Choice B — "Consisting primarily of letters sent from a young man named Little Dog to his mother, Rose, Ocean Vuong's On Earth We're Briefly Gorgeous is an epistolary novel.": This names the novel, identifies it explicitly as epistolary, and describes its letter-based format — all three elements needed for a clear example. ✓
  • Choice C — "It was Ocean Vuong who published the novel On Earth We're Briefly Gorgeous in 2019.": This names the novel but says nothing about its format or that it is epistolary. ✗
  • Choice D — "An epistolary novel is a novel written primarily as a series of fictional documents, such as letters, journal entries, or newspaper clippings.": This gives a definition, not an example of a specific epistolary novel. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
